tool: Siril 1.4.4 (register/seqapplyreg/stack/findstar/platesolve), astrometry.net via scripts/calibrate/solve_field.py, exiftool. Radial binning of Siril's PSF fits by scripts/qa/star_shape_profile.py.
purpose: Wide-field UNTRACKED registration defect: global star alignment (single homography) leaves the centre sharp and smears edge stars. Root-cause + candidate fix, tested on the real frames.

field.note: nominal lens marking is 70 mm; the solved scale implies ~67.8 mm

mount_verification.claim: fixed tripod (acquisition.json declares mount=fixed) — independently confirmed
mount_verification.mechanism: a direction fixed in the rotating Earth frame traces a rotation about the polar axis, which preserves declination and advances RA at the sidereal rate

floor_in_exposure_trailing.measured_from: Siril findstar per-frame fits over all 373 frames: roundness (FWHMy/FWHMx) 0.615 median, UNIFORM across the set (0.589-0.675), FWHM 3.634 px
floor_in_exposure_trailing.consequence: NO registration method can remove this - it is within-exposure. Stars are elongated ~1.6:1 at best. Success = the edge matching the centre, not round stars.
floor_in_exposure_trailing.corollary: per-frame roundness is uniform across the set, so the radius-dependent smear is introduced by register+stack, NOT by the frames

root_cause.ruled_out: field rotation + gnomonic projection. For an IDEAL rectilinear lens a pure camera rotation maps EXACTLY to an 8-DOF homography (Szeliski, Image Alignment and Stitching, sec. 2.3) - stars are at infinity and sky rotation is SO(3), linear in homogeneous coords. Zero residual. This was the assumed cause and it is wrong.
root_cause.actual: unmodelled RADIAL LENS DISTORTION. The real map is distort o H o distort^-1, which is not a homography. NIKKOR Z 24-70/4 S measures ~3.4% pincushion at 70 mm (~120 px at the r=3643 px corner). As a star drifts 1500 px it samples a different local distortion and no global 8-DOF fit absorbs the difference.
root_cause.asymmetry_unresolved: the pattern is predominantly radial but carries a genuine one-sided component (at comparable radius, right majFWHM 9.66 vs left 5.66). A best-fit radial centre lands only 87 px from the crop centre (rank-corr 0.320 -> 0.331), so an off-centre crop does NOT explain it. Candidates: differential refraction (asymmetric with hour angle, ~1-4 px) and lens decentering. UNRESOLVED.

experiment.design: 54 lights = every 7th of 373, spanning the FULL 43-min window (the residual scales with TIME SPAN, not frame count). Master dark + validated sky flat, -cfa -equalize_cfa -debayer (debayer BEFORE registration), -framing=min, stack rej 3 3 -norm=addscale. ONE knob: -disto=.
experiment.measures_from: Siril findstar with the roundness gate opened (-roundness=0.05 -relax=on) so elongated stars are DETECTED rather than silently rejected
experiment.verdict_B: LOSS. -disto= did not remove the defect; it relocated and worsened it, smearing the whole frame. Star density -57% (1259 -> 541 stars/Mpx) at near-identical frame size. Hypothesis killed with numbers.
experiment.verdict_C: CONFIRMS THE MECHANISM. Better at EVERY radius; its inner field (r<1700) sits exactly at the single-frame floor (roundness 0.619-0.634 vs the 0.615 floor; majFWHM 3.52 vs the 3.63 per-frame FWHM) - registration adds nothing there. Remove the drift and the homography becomes exact. Cost: 12 frames vs 54. C ALSO keeps 56% more field (22.05 vs 14.11 Mpx) because less drift means -framing=min intersects away less.
experiment.measurement_trap: TWO traps, both hit in this experiment. (1) SURVIVORSHIP BIAS: B's radial profile appears to IMPROVE at the edge (4.61 vs A's 6.46) because B's smear pushed most stars below the detection threshold, so the median is over the lucky survivors - the full-frame crops settle it. (2) AREA CONFOUND: raw star counts are NOT comparable across variants because -framing=min gives each a different frame size - C's higher raw count (26354 vs 17770) is entirely its 56% larger frame; per unit area C is slightly LOWER (1195 vs 1259 stars/Mpx), exactly as 4.5x less integration predicts. Read star-shape medians with their n, and n per unit area.
experiment.artifacts_gitignored: reg/final_A.fit, reg/final_B.fit, reg/final_C.fit, reg/star_shapes_AB.png, reg/stars_{A,B,C}.lst, reg/profile_{A,B,C}.json

why_B_failed.mechanism_is_sound: register seq -2pass -disto=file <path> -> 'Distortion data is valid and will be used'; seqapplyreg -> 'Distortion data was found in the sequence file, undistortion will be applied'. The -2pass + seqapplyreg chain DOES carry the undistortion to export even though -disto= is absent from seqapplyreg's own help. Syntax: '-disto=file <path>' (two tokens); '-disto=file=<path>' errors; '-disto=image' needs the loaded image solved. Siril also READS an astrometry.net-injected TAN-SIP header it did not write.
why_B_failed.model_is_the_broken_link: the camera is on a fixed tripod, so lens distortion is physically IDENTICAL in every frame. Two independent astrometry.net solves 43 min apart disagree at the same sensor positions by a MEDIAN of 65.3 px (worst 127.9). A real lens model must agree to ~1 px.
why_B_failed.more_stars_do_not_fix_it: --max-stars=1500 cut the SIP disagreement only to 43.8 px median (worst 132.1), while the LINEAR solve improved sharply: scale 18.02/18.20 -> 18.05/18.06, RA-drift error 6% -> 0.3%, logodds 127 -> 782. More stars fix the position, not the distortion.
why_B_failed.mechanism_of_that_failure: astrometry.net's SIP tweak is constrained by MATCHED INDEX stars; the 4200-series index at the wide scales this field needs (12-19) is Tycho-2-based and sparse. Supplying more FIELD stars cannot help when few index stars exist to match.
why_B_failed.also_blocks: the WCS-reprojection route (SWarp / astropy reproject / Montage) needs the same trustworthy per-frame distortion solution, so it inherits this blocker.

siril_solver_cannot_supply_it.result: reproduces AND refines the dead-end. With the correct centre, local Gaia and -nocrop, Siril reports 'Initial solve failed' -> near-solve failed at a computed FOV of 36.45 deg (diagonal).
siril_solver_cannot_supply_it.eliminated_catalogue_depth: -limitmag=+4 raised the fetch 2177 -> 138498 Gaia stars (limit mag 7.81 -> 11.81); still failed
siril_solver_cannot_supply_it.conclusion: the blocker is Siril's star MATCHER at ultra-wide FOV, not the roundness gate and not the auto limit magnitude
siril_solver_cannot_supply_it.side_finding: Siril's auto limit magnitude for a 36 deg field is 7.81, while its detection goes far deeper - a real population mismatch worth knowing even though it is not the blocker

the_model_exists_in_the_data.meaning: 'On (Required)' = the raw is UNCORRECTED and the profile is meant to be applied downstream. The exact lens model ships in every frame.
the_model_exists_in_the_data.supersedes: research sources state the Nikon Z correction block is undecoded by open source and unreachable via exiv2 (discuss.pixls.us, 2023). The on-rig probe contradicts this for the radial terms - exiftool decodes them today. Doc-vs-binary conflict resolved by the probe.
the_model_exists_in_the_data.the_gap: no headless Linux tool APPLIES the profile (and the exact model form / normalisation is undocumented). That single missing link is what stands between this data and the proven -disto= mechanism.

lensfun_experiment.verdict: WIN — the first route that fixes the defect. MECHANISM PROOF, not a shippable render (see limits).
lensfun_experiment.question: does an OFFICIAL lens distortion model (rather than a SIP fitted from sparse trailed stars) flatten roundness-vs-radius?
lensfun_experiment.tools: darktable 5.4.1 (built with Lensfun 0.3.4) + lensfun upstream DB via lensfun-update-data. darktable does every pixel op; lensfun owns the model and its normalisation.
lensfun_experiment.why_this_source_works: the model is MEASURED for the lens rather than fitted per-frame from the data, so it is immune to the index-sparsity failure that made the astrometry.net SIP non-reproducible.

profile.model: ptlens a=0.012 b=-0.017 c=0.039 (focal=70 is a CALIBRATED entry, not interpolated)
profile.db_note: Debian's lensfun 0.3.4 DB does NOT contain the Z6III; `lensfun-update-data` installs the upstream DB to ~/.local/share/lensfun/updates/version_1, which HAS `Nikon Z6_3`. Without that update the camera cannot match and no correction is possible.

lensfun_experiment.design: 54 lights = every 7th of 373, FULL 43-min window. ONE knob: the darktable lens module's enabled bit. Both arms run --style-overwrite with an IDENTICAL module set (styles `lensfix` enabled=1 vs `nolens` enabled=0), so histories differ only in that bit.
lensfun_experiment.result: roundness 0.550 -> 0.656 (+19%); majFWHM 4.63 -> 4.25 px; star DENSITY 981 -> 1418 /Mpx (+45%, and ON's frame is 3% SMALLER so this is not the area artifact); roundness FLAT across radius (0.674/0.683/0.655/0.631/0.659 from r=448 to r=2685) where OFF sags to 0.556 with majFWHM spiking to 5.19. Two frames that could not match now register (52/54 -> 54/54). Crops: reg/star_shapes_lensfun.png.
lensfun_experiment.mechanism_confirmed_not_just_outcome: the CENTRE bin also improved (roundness 0.507 -> 0.594) where distortion is ~0. That is what theory predicts: with the frames undistorted, ONE global homography fits every star instead of being a compromise that degrades everywhere. It also explains the two newly-matchable frames.
lensfun_experiment.confound_declared: the preset carries modify_flags=7 = distortion|TCA|vignetting, so vignetting and TCA correction were applied too. Vignetting cannot change star SHAPE, but it brightens the corners and therefore inflates part of the star-COUNT gain via extra detections. The roundness/FWHM gains are shape measures and are not affected; the centre-bin gain (where vignetting ~1.0) is uncontaminated. A clean distortion-only re-run (modify_flags=1) is BACKLOG.
limits: NOT a shippable render: darktable works from raw, so this arm carries NO darks/flats (dark/flat calibration must happen in sensor space BEFORE any geometric warp). It is a GEOMETRY test; the defect is flat-independent, which is what makes it valid.
Depth differs slightly (52 vs 54 frames) — far too small to explain a 45% density gain.
The profile is a COMMUNITY measurement of the lens model, not Nikon's own coefficients (which ship in every NEF but live in a Nikon-private SubIFD block no headless Linux tool applies).
Absolute numbers are NOT comparable with the A/B/C experiments above: different pipeline (darktable 16-bit vs Siril CFA->debayer 32-bit). Only OFF-vs-ON is a valid comparison.
lensfun_experiment.productionisation_required: Siril calibrate (CFA, dark+sky flat) -> debayer -> apply the distortion warp -> register -> stack. The open question is applying the lensfun warp to a calibrated linear image rather than to raw.

route_decision.SUPERSEDED_BY: lensfun_experiment - a distortion model from an OFFICIAL measured profile (darktable+lensfun) fixes the defect at full 43-min depth. The depth-vs-edge trade-off below is no longer forced.
route_decision.recommended_today: the lensfun distortion model, productionised (see lensfun_experiment.productionisation_required). It removes the edge degradation at FULL depth, which routes A and C could not both do.
route_decision.superseded_route_A: global homography, debayer-first, full 43-min depth (majFWHM 4.74 px whole-frame) - the best result available BEFORE a distortion model existed. Its edge degradation was a known, quantified defect.
route_decision.not_proposed: cropping to the good field. That hides a defect that is in the data.
route_decision.superseded_route_C: short window - bought floor-limited sharpness for ~4x less integration. Only needed while no distortion model existed; keep as the fallback if productionisation fails.
route_decision.the_fix: PROVEN (lensfun_experiment). Remaining work is ORDERING, not discovery: calibrate in sensor space, then warp, then register. Ranked in BACKLOG.
route_decision.gate: user confirms on own viewer, full-frame lossless finals, dust-preservation the deciding metric

trade_offs: Route A ships full depth with a measured edge defect; route C ships a floor-limited frame at 1/4 depth. Neither is the fix; the fix needs a tool this rig does not have.

Short-window sub-stacking does NOT compose: combining the sub-stacks reintroduces the identical model error (mapping block k to block 1 spans the same drift) and turns a smooth smear into a few discrete ghosts.

The existing 124-frame baseline registered UNDEBAYERED CFA data through lanczos4, which mixes neighbouring R/G/B photosites (its previews carry no real colour). Variant A debayers first and measures better (majFWHM 5.25 -> 4.74 px) - but frame count and CFA sampling confound a strict comparison, so this is SUGGESTIVE, not proven.
caveats: 54 of 373 frames (every 7th) for disk/time; the FULL time span is preserved, which is what the geometry depends on. Depth is ~1/7 of a production stack.
arm base rig; Siril 1.4.4 is the IDENTICAL tool on the x86 target, so the method transfers (output is not bit-identical).
The one-sided component of the defect is unresolved (refraction vs decentering).
Route A vs C finals have not yet been judged by the user's eyes.

linearity_of_the_warp_round_trip.why_it_matters: the production chain round-trips Siril-calibrated LINEAR data through darktable. A non-linear round trip silently corrupts photometry, SPCC and the stretch, and looks fine on a preview.
linearity_of_the_warp_round_trip.trap_found: Siril savetif embeds sRGB-elle-V2-srgbtrc.icc (an sRGB TONE CURVE) on LINEAR pixels. icc_assign sRGBlinear does NOT change what savetif embeds, and `set gui.icc_pedantic_linear=true` does not either — the export profile comes from a save-time preference.

measured_BROKEN.verdict: NON-LINEAR — the sRGB decode is applied and never cancelled

measured_FIXED.config: --icc-type SRGB (output profile MATCHED to the input tag)
measured_FIXED.verdict: IDENTITY round trip — colorin decode and colorout/gamma encode cancel exactly

linearity_of_the_warp_round_trip.method: Siril findstar amplitude (A) of the same stars before/after a NO-OP round trip (lens module disabled), binned by brightness. A linear round trip gives a CONSTANT ratio; a gamma bends it. Never test linearity with a mean or a preview.
linearity_of_the_warp_round_trip.rule: MATCH the output profile to whatever tag the input carries; do not try to force a linear tag. The tag is wrong either way — it only has to be wrong consistently.

production_experiment.verdict: WIN — CONFIRMED on the real production chain. Distortion-only, calibration intact, linearity verified.
production_experiment.chain: Siril calibrate (CFA, master dark + validated sky flat) -> debayer -> savetif -> exiftool (Make/Model/LensModel/FocalLength; darktable needs them to match the profile) -> darktable-cli --style lensdist --style-overwrite --icc-type SRGB -> Siril register -2pass -> seqapplyreg -framing=min -> stack
production_experiment.one_knob: the lens module's enabled bit (styles `lensdist` enabled=1 vs `nodist` enabled=0; identical op_params otherwise)
production_experiment.distortion_only: modify_flags patched 7 -> 1 in darktable's own blob: DISTORTION only, no vignetting (which would fight the sky flat) and no TCA
production_experiment.linearity: --icc-type SRGB matched to the sRGB-TRC tag savetif embeds; VERIFIED identity round trip (A_out/A_in 0.9996-1.0000, IQR 0.0003)
production_experiment.result: roundness 0.551 -> 0.643 (+17%); majFWHM 4.42 -> 4.06 px; star DENSITY 1010 -> 1610 /Mpx (+59%; ON's frame is only 1.6% larger, so the area trap is checked). The control's radial degradation (majFWHM 3.99 -> 6.20, +55% across the field) is largely removed: ON holds 3.91-4.23 px over the bulk of the field, 5.14 px in the outermost bin.
production_experiment.corrects_earlier_caution: the vignetting confound flagged in lensfun_experiment did NOT drive the star-count gain: with vignetting REMOVED the gain is LARGER (+59% vs +45%). Sharper stars concentrate flux above the detection threshold. The confound was isolated, not assumed.
open: ON's innermost bin (r<445, n=386 only) reads roundness 0.523 / majFWHM 4.55 — worse than its neighbours (0.644/4.02) and worse than OFF's centre. Small-n, but UNEXPLAINED; check before trusting the very centre.

The outermost bin is still elevated (5.14 vs 3.91 px mid-field) — the correction is large but not total. Residual candidates: the community profile's accuracy, and the unresolved one-sided component (refraction / decentering).
DUST GATE NOT RUN: priority #1. The warp resamples every pixel; whether it costs faint IFN is the user's eyes on FULL-FRAME LOSSLESS finals, with/without.

deepest_render.verdict: SHIPPED — dust gate PASSED on the user's eyes (dust preserved, deep red intact, centre and right fixed).
deepest_render.why_168: DISK ceiling, not a choice: 231 MB/frame uncompressed (register needs the whole input set resident while seqapplyreg writes the output beside it) => 84 GB for 373 vs ~41 GB free. No compression (user rule: setcompress quantisation is lossless on int16 but silently lossy on float). Stride preserves the TIME SPAN, which is what the geometry depends on; only depth is traded.
deepest_render.lost_frame: 169th lost to the chunk-remainder bug (Siril cannot build a sequence from 1 frame) — 0.3% of integration; bug recorded in BACKLOG.
deepest_render.reproduces_the_54_frame_result: roundness 0.643 -> 0.637, majFWHM 4.06 -> 4.07 across 3.1x the frames. The correction is a property of the OPTICS and the route, not of which frames are in the stack — a real check, not a restatement.
known_defects_carried: The stride is QUALITY-BLIND: this stack contains DSC_6900 (flagged 'worst on all three axes') by accident. 1 frame in 168 at 6% spread — not visible, but an unrecorded default. Culling is a BACKLOG item for a fresh session, deliberately NOT patched mid-render.
Innermost bin (r<445) reads WORSE than its neighbours — UNEXPLAINED, see innermost_bin_anomaly.
Outermost bin still elevated (5.11 vs 3.88 px mid-field): the correction is large but not total.

innermost_bin_anomaly.status: OPEN — reproduced, not explained. Flagged for a fresh session.
innermost_bin_anomaly.observation: In BOTH distortion-corrected runs the INNERMOST radial bin is the WORST of the frame; in the CONTROL it is the BEST. So the correction appears to make the centre worse, where distortion is ~0.
innermost_bin_anomaly.the_sharper_clue: star COUNT in that bin collapses: control 992 vs corrected 386/423 (~2.3x fewer) over a near-identical pixel area. darktable's autoscale (1.046) magnifies by only 4.6% and cannot explain a 2.3x drop.
innermost_bin_anomaly.not_noise: reproduced independently in two runs (54 and 168 frames, different frame subsets) and absent from the control.
candidate_causes_to_test: The radial binning centre is the STAR BOUNDING-BOX centre (scripts/qa/star_shape_profile.py default), not the optical axis or darktable's distortion centre. If the warp moves the field, the assumed centre may be wrong — test with --center= at the true optical axis.
darktable's lens module autoscale (scale=1.046) — a uniform magnification; check whether it interacts with the innermost region or the ROI change (6064x4040 -> 6056x4036 -> back).
A lens-module artifact at/near the distortion centre (the polynomial's origin).
Small-n statistics: n=386-423 is the smallest bin, but the CONTROL's same bin has n=992, so a pure small-n explanation must also explain the count collapse.
innermost_bin_anomaly.why_it_matters: if real, the route is degrading the sharpest part of the frame while fixing the edges — a trade nobody agreed to. If it is a measurement artifact of the binning centre, the metric is wrong and every radial profile in this deep-dive inherits it.
